D9 BiologyL6 CreateReference

Beetle Cholesteric Helicoid — Circularly Polarised Reflection

Plusiotis (Chrysina) beetles reflect left-circularly-polarised metallic gold/green via a cholesteric chitin helicoid — pitch matches half-wavelength, handedness encodes which polarisation reflects

Cholesteric (Bragg) chitin layers rotate by a small angle from one layer to the next, forming a helical structure. The pitch p sets the Bragg condition λ = n_avg × p (only matching circularly-polarised light reflects). Same-handedness as the helix reflects; opposite handedness transmits.

Drag the pitch; the metallic reflection colour scans across the visible.
